none.justification: Correctly identifies `raw_spinlock_t __lock` at `sched.h:964`, explains the `rq_lockp`/core-scheduling indirection and retrying lock helpers, and gives valid acquisition sites (`core.c:794` and `core.c:2601`). Minor line off-by-one on wrapper names is not material.

ccodegraph.justification: Gets the field, main lock/unlock helpers, core-scheduling retry mechanism, and two valid call sites (`core.c:616`, `core.c:6381`). However it incorrectly describes `__rq_lockp()` as always returning `&rq->__lock` and omits the `rq_lock`/`rq_unlock` wrapper family, so it has a real helper/mechanism gap.

codegraph.justification: Matches the GT on the `__lock` field, `rq_lockp`/`__rq_lockp` indirection, retrying `raw_spin_rq_*` helpers, wrapper family, and gives valid call sites (`core.c:794`, `core.c:4027`). The line range wording is slightly broad but substantively correct.

cbm.justification: Correctly reports `__lock` at `sched.h:964`, the core-scheduling indirection, `rq_lockp`/`__rq_lockp`, `raw_spin_rq_lock_nested`/trylock/unlock helpers, and valid acquisition sites (`core.c:2689`, `core.c:6381`). It is complete enough for full credit despite not emphasizing the higher-level `rq_lock` wrapper family as much.
